name: Gan Chinese
notes: Grapheme inventory NOT YET SPECIFIED (stub). Gan is written with Chinese characters (Han script). No logographic character-to-phoneme map is claimed here: Chinese characters do not encode pronunciation systematically, so a Han-script G2P for this variety would need either a full character-reading lexicon or a romanised input. INPUT CONTRACT: this spec expects a romanisation as input, not Han characters — for Gan the relevant convention is the Nanchang-based Gan romanisation (as used by the Gan Wikipedia), in the same way that the `zh` spec expects Hanyu Pinyin, `nan` expects Pe̍h-ōe-jī / Tâi-lô, Cantonese expects Jyutping, Hakka expects Pha̍k-fa-sṳ, Mindong expects Bàng-uâ-cê and Wu expects Wugniu. Gan is tonal; tone is carried by the romanisation's diacritics or digits, not by the characters.
